artifacts.command:
ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=INFO ATOM_USE_UNIFIED_ATTN=1 ATOM_USE_TRITON_GEMM=1 AITER_ROPE_NATIVE_BACKEND=1 AITER_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_RMSNORM_QUANT=0 ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_SILU_MUL_QUANT=0 ATOM_ENABLE_ALLREDUCE_RMSNORM_FUSION=0 bash -lc '
    set -euo pipefail
    PORT=31010
    ENGINE_PORT=32010
    MODEL=/home/homelab1/datapool/ai_models/safetensors/Qwen/Qwen3-14B-FP8
    SERVER_LOG=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs/atom-r9700-qwen3-14b-fp8-smoke-pp16-tg8-tp1-rocr.server.log
    RESULT_DIR=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs
    RESULT_FILE=atom-r9700-qwen3-14b-fp8-smoke-pp16-tg8-tp1-rocr.result.json
    RESULT="$RESULT_DIR/$RESULT_FILE"
    rm -f "$RESULT" "$SERVER_LOG" "$RESULT.pytorch.json"
    setsid build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.entrypoints.openai_server \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --host 127.0.0.1 \
      --server-port "$PORT" \
      --port "$ENGINE_PORT" \
      --level 0 \
      --kv_cache_dtype bf16 \
      --block-size 64 \
      --max-model-len 4096 \
      --gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
      -tp 1 \
      > "$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
    server_pid=$!
    cleanup() {
      kill -- -"$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    }
    trap cleanup EXIT
    for _ in $(seq 1 600); do
      if ! kill -0 "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ATOM server exited before readiness" >&2
        tail -240 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if grep -qi "out of memory\|memory access fault\|trace/breakpoint trap\|segmentation fault" "$SERVER_LOG"; then
        echo "ATOM server hit fatal error before readiness" >&2
        tail -240 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null; then
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done
    curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null
    build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.benchmarks.benchmark_serving \
      --backend vllm \
      --base-url "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}" \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --tokenizer "$MODEL" \
      --dataset-name random \
      --num-prompts 1 \
      --random-input-len 16 \
      --random-output-len 8 \
      --request-rate inf \
      --disable-tqdm \
      --percentile-metrics ttft,tpot,itl,e2el \
      --metric-percentiles 50,95 \
      --save-result \
      --result-dir "$RESULT_DIR" \
      --result-filename "$RESULT_FILE"
  '

notes: smoke server+serving benchmark using ATOM gfx1201 recipe env
ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 selects R9700/gfx1201
ATOM_USE_UNIFIED_ATTN=1 and ATOM_USE_TRITON_GEMM=1 per Qwen3-8B-FP8 recipe
local ATOM fallback aliases aiter.ops.shuffle.shuffle_scale as moe_shuffle_scale for amd-aiter wheel

artifacts.command:
ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=INFO ATOM_USE_UNIFIED_ATTN=1 ATOM_USE_TRITON_GEMM=1 AITER_ROPE_NATIVE_BACKEND=1 AITER_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_RMSNORM_QUANT=0 ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_SILU_MUL_QUANT=0 ATOM_ENABLE_ALLREDUCE_RMSNORM_FUSION=0 bash -lc '
    set -euo pipefail
    PORT=31011
    ENGINE_PORT=32011
    MODEL=/home/homelab1/datapool/ai_models/safetensors/Qwen/Qwen3-14B-FP8
    CASE=atom-r9700-qwen3-14b-fp8-smoke-pp16-tg8-tp1-rocr-eager-ctx256
    SERVER_LOG=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs/${CASE}.server.log
    RESULT_DIR=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs
    RESULT_FILE=${CASE}.result.json
    RESULT="$RESULT_DIR/$RESULT_FILE"
    rm -f "$RESULT" "$SERVER_LOG" "$RESULT.pytorch.json"
    setsid build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.entrypoints.openai_server \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --host 127.0.0.1 \
      --server-port "$PORT" \
      --port "$ENGINE_PORT" \
      --level 0 \
      --enforce-eager \
      --kv_cache_dtype bf16 \
      --block-size 64 \
      --max-model-len 256 \
      --gpu-memory-utilization 0.75 \
      -tp 1 \
      > "$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
    server_pid=$!
    cleanup() {
      kill -- -"$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    }
    trap cleanup EXIT
    for _ in $(seq 1 600); do
      if ! kill -0 "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ATOM server exited before readiness" >&2
        tail -240 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if grep -qi "out of memory\|memory access fault\|trace/breakpoint trap\|segmentation fault" "$SERVER_LOG"; then
        echo "ATOM server hit fatal error before readiness" >&2
        tail -240 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null; then
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done
    curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null
    build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.benchmarks.benchmark_serving \
      --backend vllm \
      --base-url "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}" \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --tokenizer "$MODEL" \
      --dataset-name random \
      --num-prompts 1 \
      --random-input-len 16 \
      --random-output-len 8 \
      --request-rate inf \
      --disable-tqdm \
      --percentile-metrics ttft,tpot,itl,e2el \
      --metric-percentiles 50,95 \
      --save-result \
      --result-dir "$RESULT_DIR" \
      --result-filename "$RESULT_FILE"
  '

notes: smoke eager server+serving benchmark using ATOM gfx1201 recipe env

local ATOM fallback aliases aiter.ops.shuffle.shuffle_scale as moe_shuffle_scale for amd-aiter wheel

artifacts.command:
ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=INFO ATOM_USE_UNIFIED_ATTN=1 ATOM_USE_TRITON_GEMM=1 AITER_ROPE_NATIVE_BACKEND=1 AITER_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_RMSNORM_QUANT=0 ATOM_LLAMA_ENABLE_AITER_TRITON_FUSED_SILU_MUL_QUANT=0 ATOM_ENABLE_ALLREDUCE_RMSNORM_FUSION=0 bash -lc '
    set -euo pipefail
    PORT=31013
    ENGINE_PORT=32013
    MODEL=/home/homelab1/datapool/ai_models/safetensors/Qwen/Qwen3-14B-FP8
    CASE=atom-r9700-qwen3-14b-fp8-smoke-pp16-tg8-tp1-rocr-aiter-src-eager-ctx256-mbt256
    SERVER_LOG=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs/${CASE}.server.log
    RESULT_DIR=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs
    RESULT_FILE=${CASE}.result.json
    RESULT="$RESULT_DIR/$RESULT_FILE"
    rm -f "$RESULT" "$SERVER_LOG" "$RESULT.pytorch.json"
    setsid build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.entrypoints.openai_server \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --host 127.0.0.1 \
      --server-port "$PORT" \
      --port "$ENGINE_PORT" \
      --level 0 \
      --enforce-eager \
      --kv_cache_dtype bf16 \
      --block-size 64 \
      --max-model-len 256 \
      --max-num-batched-tokens 256 \
      --max-num-seqs 1 \
      --gpu-memory-utilization 0.75 \
      -tp 1 \
      > "$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
    server_pid=$!
    cleanup() {
      kill -- -"$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    }
    trap cleanup EXIT
    for _ in $(seq 1 900); do
      if ! kill -0 "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ATOM server exited before readiness" >&2
        tail -300 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if grep -qi "out of memory\|memory access fault\|trace/breakpoint trap\|segmentation fault" "$SERVER_LOG"; then
        echo "ATOM server hit fatal error before readiness" >&2
        tail -300 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null; then
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done
    curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null
    build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.benchmarks.benchmark_serving \
      --backend vllm \
      --base-url "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}" \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --tokenizer "$MODEL" \
      --dataset-name random \
      --num-prompts 1 \
      --random-input-len 16 \
      --random-output-len 8 \
      --request-rate inf \
      --disable-tqdm \
      --percentile-metrics ttft,tpot,itl,e2el \
      --metric-percentiles 50,95 \
      --save-result \
      --result-dir "$RESULT_DIR" \
      --result-filename "$RESULT_FILE"
  '

    MODEL=/home/homelab1/datapool/ai_models/safetensors/Qwen/Qwen3-14B-FP8
    CASE=atom-r9700-qwen3-14b-fp8-official-ish-pp549-tg256-cudagraph-fp8kv-ctx16384-util90-p99
    SERVER_LOG=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs/${CASE}.server.log
    RESULT_DIR=benchmarks/results/2026-06-30/atom/logs
    RESULT_FILE=${CASE}.result.json
    RESULT="$RESULT_DIR/$RESULT_FILE"
    rm -f "$RESULT" "$SERVER_LOG" "$RESULT.pytorch.json"
    setsid build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.entrypoints.openai_server \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --host 127.0.0.1 \
      --server-port "$PORT" \
      --port "$ENGINE_PORT" \
      --level 0 \
      --kv_cache_dtype fp8 \
      --block-size 64 \
      --max-model-len 16384 \
      --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 \
      -tp 1 \
      > "$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
    server_pid=$!
    cleanup() {
      kill -- -"$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    }
    trap cleanup EXIT
    for _ in $(seq 1 900); do
      if ! kill -0 "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ATOM server exited before readiness" >&2
        tail -300 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if grep -qi "out of memory\|memory access fault\|trace/breakpoint trap\|segmentation fault" "$SERVER_LOG"; then
        echo "ATOM server hit fatal error before readiness" >&2
        tail -300 "$SERVER_LOG" >&2 || true
        exit 1
      fi
      if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null; then
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done
    curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/health" >/dev/null
    build/envs/atom-rocm/bin/python -m atom.benchmarks.benchmark_serving \
      --backend vllm \
      --base-url "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}" \
      --model "$MODEL" \
      --tokenizer "$MODEL" \
      --dataset-name random \
      --num-prompts 1 \
      --random-input-len 549 \
      --random-output-len 256 \
      --request-rate inf \
      --disable-tqdm \
      --percentile-metrics ttft,tpot,itl,e2el \
      --metric-percentiles 50,95,99 \
      --save-result \
      --result-dir "$RESULT_DIR" \
      --result-filename "$RESULT_FILE"
  '

notes: cause isolation: Qwen3-14B-FP8 official-like workload with cudagraph, FP8 KV, gpu memory utilization 0.9
compares model-size effect after reproducing Qwen3-8B TPOT-class official speed
